rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: The overall aim of this course is for students to acquire the knowledge that they need to succeed in the IELTS and TOEFL tests. This includes knowledge of the test format, and the the comprehension and productive skills on which they are tested. Students will also learn the techniques that are used to produce the tests. Practical exercises in all areas will help to reinforce this knowledge.
aims: Acquiring the knowledge needed to succeed in the IELTS and TOEFL tests.
goals: Students will develop the ability to use the reading skills and strategies that are tested in IELTS and TOEFL
Students will develop the ability to use the listening skills and strategies that are tested in IELTS and TOEFL
Students will develop the ability to use the writing skills and strategies that are tested in IELTS and TOEFL
Students will develop the ability to use the speaking skills and strategies that are tested in IELTS and TOEFL
Students will work to acquire the academic vocabulary that is used in IELTS and TOEFL
Students will develop a thorough understanding of the purposes of academic English tests, and the techniques that are used to produce them.
prereq: Students registering for this course should have acquired (or have language proficiency equivalent to) EIKEN 2nd grade. They must also be prepared to commit to preparation outside of class.
prep: In order to succeed in the IELTS and TOEFL tests an examinee requires a good command of vocabuary. Students are therefore required to complete weekly online vocabulary study assignments, which will be part of their final grade. They must also complete other assignments, as required.
teachers: ◎ マイケル シャープ

教員の実務経験との関連
The instructor has been teaching and researching academic English exams for many years. This experience has provided an in-depth understanding of the methods used to prepare the tests, the specific skills that are being tested and the specific strategies that test takers need to use.
